close_reason: Replaced panic payload extraction with static string
description: sign_sync() captures the panic message string from the signing thread (lines 72-82 in lib.rs) and embeds it verbatim in SecretError::Backend { source }. SecretError derives Debug and its Display format includes the source chain. If a backend panics with a string containing sensitive data (key identifier, auth credential, partial key material), that string flows into error values that callers may log, include in HTTP responses, or surface in tracing spans. The adapter amplifies the risk by explicitly extracting and re-emitting the panic payload rather than substituting a static message. Confidentiality risk: sensitive strings from backend panics may leak into logs or error responses. Fix: replace the captured panic string with a static 'signing thread panicked' message and discard the payload, or document that backends must not panic with sensitive data.
title: Panic payload from signing thread forwarded unfiltered
